prompt:
DB-platform ticket, medium priority. Our nightly capacity job resizes Atlas clusters by shelling out to the Atlas CLI and grepping its stdout; last Tuesday it re-issued a resize every minute for an hour because it couldn't tell UPDATING from IDLE, and it once recreated a cluster that was mid-DELETE. Replace it with a real Python reconciler package: atlas_clusters (stdlib only, repo root, split it into sensible modules — client / reconcile / errors is how I'd cut it, your call). test_atlas_clusters.py is the acceptance contract.

Do the research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official MongoDB documentation for the Atlas Administration API v2 clusters resource — at minimum the create-cluster and get-cluster operation pages and the versioned-API overview. Pay close attention to which dated media type each operation currently takes (reads and writes on this resource do NOT share one version date — that detail is a hard test), the exact replicationSpecs/regionConfigs/electableSpecs payload shape, the stateName lifecycle, and the error envelope. Only first-party MongoDB pages count as sources (mongodb.com/docs, the Atlas Admin API v2 reference, or MongoDB's own SDK/spec repos). Our pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json — both protected. If live docs disagree with the fixtures, say so in your summary and leave fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Surface the tests import:
- AtlasClient(base_url, token) — sends Authorization: Bearer <token> and the documented dated Accept media type on every call; writes also send the documented dated Content-Type.
- ClusterReconciler(client, sleep, poll_interval) — all waiting goes through the injected sleep(seconds); never time.sleep.
- ClusterReconciler.reconcile(group_id, desired) with desired = {name, clusterType, mongoDBMajorVersion, replicationSpecs:[{zoneName, regionConfigs:[{providerName, regionName, priority, electableSpecs:{instanceSize, nodeCount, diskSizeGB}}]}]}:
  * GET the cluster. A 404 CLUSTER_NOT_FOUND means create it (POST, body exactly the desired payload). Present-but-different means PATCH with ONLY the changed piece (the tests pin {"replicationSpecs": [...]}). Present-and-matching means do nothing — no write, no polling, action "unchanged". Compare only the fields we own; ignore everything the server adds (ids, zoneId, stateName, connectionStrings, links, ...).
  * After a write, poll the GET operation, sleeping poll_interval before each poll, until stateName reaches the state meaning "all changes applied" per the docs. Return {"action": "created"|"updated"|"unchanged", "final": <last cluster document>}.
  * Stop on terminal failure: if a poll shows the cluster DELETING, or the cluster 404s away mid-poll, raise ClusterStateError (with a state_name attribute, None when it vanished) and issue no further requests. Never blind-retry a 4xx.
- AtlasApiError raised for non-2xx ApiError responses, with attributes status, error_code, detail, parameters, reason. A 409 duplicate-name create is terminal: exactly one POST, no retry. No exception text may contain the bearer token.

Acceptance: python3 test_atlas_clusters.py — it runs a loopback mock that 406s any request whose Accept doesn't name the current version of that specific operation, exactly like Atlas. No real Atlas, no credentials, no sleeps. test_atlas_clusters.py and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: Atlas Administration API v2 Clusters resource — GET media type 2024-08-05, POST/PATCH media type 2024-10-23 (ClusterDescription20240805)

official_sources.title: Create One Cluster in One Project | Atlas Admin API v2 documentation
official_sources.used_for: POST /api/atlas/v2/groups/{groupId}/clusters; request body name/clusterType (REPLICASET|SHARDED|GEOSHARDED)/mongoDBMajorVersion/replicationSpecs[{zoneName, regionConfigs[{providerName, regionName, priority, electableSpecs{instanceSize, nodeCount, diskSizeGB}}]}]; 201 response with id/stateName; declared errors 400/401/402/403/404/409 Conflict/429/500 with the {error, errorCode, detail, parameters, reason, badRequestDetail} body

official_sources.title: Return One Cluster From One Project | Atlas Admin API v2 documentation
MongoDB Atlas Administration API 2.0 — official OpenAPI specification (latest resource versions)
official_sources.used_for: per-operation dated media types: getGroupCluster 200 is application/vnd.atlas.2024-08-05+json while createGroupCluster/updateGroupCluster request+response are application/vnd.atlas.2024-10-23+json; ClusterDescription20240805 and ReplicationSpec20240805 {id, zoneId, zoneName, regionConfigs} with readOnly id/zoneId; stateName enum IDLE/CREATING/UPDATING/DELETING/REPAIRING; ApiError served as application/json

official_sources.title: Versioned Atlas Administration API Overview - Atlas - MongoDB Docs
official_sources.used_for: Accept: application/vnd.atlas.<yyyy-mm-dd>+json format; resource-level versioning (operations move dates independently); 406 Not Acceptable for missing/nonexistent version dates

official_sources.title: Atlas Administration API Reference - Atlas - MongoDB Docs
official_sources.used_for: error document fields {detail, error, errorCode, parameters, reason} and their meanings

research.language_rationale: The deployment automation this reconciler joins is Python. MongoDB ships no official Atlas Administration SDK for Python — the documented integration surface for Python shops is the versioned REST API itself — so a stdlib urllib.request client that pins the dated media types explicitly is the credible, idiomatic path and keeps the version-per-operation subtlety visible.
